description_long: This is a bronze-colored Corinthian-style helmet with a pronounced crest running from front to back, reminiscent of those worn by ancient Greek warriors. The helmet features a full-face design with a T-shaped opening for the eyes and mouth, providing both protection and visibility. The surface is weathered and slightly tarnished, giving it an aged, battle-worn appearance. The crest is black and appears to be made of a different material, possibly horsehair or a synthetic substitute, and is mounted along the top ridge. The helmet's sides extend downward to protect the cheeks, while the back flares out to shield the neck. There are no visible logos, graphics, or modern attachments, emphasizing its historical and utilitarian design.
description: A weathered bronze Corinthian helmet with a black crest, full-face protection, and cheek guards, evoking ancient Greek warrior armor.
description_view_0: Front view shows the T-shaped eye and mouth opening, symmetrical cheek guards, and the central crest.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the helmet's curved profile, extended cheek guard, and the full length of the black crest.
description_view_2: Top view displays the prominent black crest running from front to back, with the helmet's rounded dome visible.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the cheek guard, neck protection, and the crest's attachment.
